author: Sebastian Hanke
description: Real AI agent for your vault. Coworker, Copilot & thinking partner, that maintains your memory & knowledge, adapts to your workflows, uses plugins, skills & tools with full safety controls. BYOK & MCP - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Sebastian Hanke
manifest.description: Agentic AI operating layer for your vault. Discovers and uses installed plugins, maintains persistent unified memory, and adapts to your workflows, skills and tools with full safety controls.
manifest.name: Vault Operator

name: Vault Operator

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Optional assets for Vault Operator v2.14.8.

These files are downloaded on demand by the plugin only after
an explicit user click in Settings:

- `ort-wasm-simd-threaded.wasm` -- semantic reranker (~12 MB)
- `plugin-source.json` -- self-development source bundle (~5 MB)
- `office-bundle.js` -- exceljs + docx + pptxgenjs (~1.7 MB)
- `pdfjs-bundle.js` -- pdfjs-dist + worker (~1.6 MB)

They are NOT part of the plugin's main release tag so the
Obsidian plugin installer never sees them. SHA-256 hashes are
pinned in the plugin code (`assetHashes.ts`) and verified
before any file is persisted.
releases.name: 2.14.8 -- Optional assets

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Vault Operator v2.14.8

Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → `https://github.com/pssah4/vault-operator`

Or install manually:
1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` into `.obsidian/plugins/vault-operator/`
2. Reload Obsidian

### Optional assets
Four optional downloads (semantic reranker, self-development source
bundle, office document support, PDF parser) are published as a
separate tag `2.14.8-assets` and installed on demand
from the plugin's Settings, never silently. The plugin works
without any of them; the affected tool reports "not installed"
until you click Install.

---
title: Obsilo v2.6
---

# Obsilo v2.6 -- Skill Packages, Universal Import, Vault Health at a Glance

Released 2026-04-19. First minor bump since v2.5: brings the Anthropic-compatible skill-package format into Obsilo, replaces the single-file skill import with a universal picker, sharpens the sidebar with a stethoscope-style vault-health indicator, and closes a dozen bugs that community testers surfaced between v2.5.3 and v2.6.0.

## Highlights

### Anthropic-compatible skill packages (EPIC-022)

Obsilo now understands the full Anthropic skill folder format: a skill is a folder with `SKILL.md` and optional `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` subfolders. Nested paths inside those sidecars (e.g. `assets/templates/master.potx`, `assets/icons/pack/icon.svg`) are supported end to end. Existing single-file skills keep working unchanged.

The loader exposes every sidecar file in the system prompt as a "next step" hint -- scripts marked sandbox-executable, references marked "read on demand", assets marked static -- so the agent knows what to reach for without inlining file contents into the prompt.

### Universal skill import

One Import button in Settings -> Skills handles every shape a skill can arrive in:

- Single `.md` file (existing flow).
- Folder picked via the native Electron dialog, scanned for `SKILL.md` and whitelisted sidecars.
- `.skill` or `.zip` archive extracted through JSZip with path-traversal rejection, whitelist filter, and a 100 MB zip-bomb guard.

When a skill already exists, a Replace / Cancel modal prompts before overwrite.

### Prefix split: `/` `#` `§`

The chat input now uses one prefix per category:

- `/<slug>` runs a skill (Claude-Code muscle memory).
- `#<slug>` inserts a custom prompt template.
- `§<slug>` runs a workflow.

The autocomplete dropdown filters strictly by prefix. The `+` button below the chat opens a searchable command picker, anchored like the `@`-file picker, so the whole skill/prompt/workflow library stays discoverable without typing anything.

### Inline `@`-references

Picking a note from the `@` autocomplete now keeps the `@<basename>` token inline in the textarea. Writing `Lies @Meeting-Notes und fasse die Entscheidungen zusammen` now reads naturally, and the attachment chip above the input still populates.

### Vault-health indicator

A stethoscope icon replaces the severity dot next to the sidebar title. Red for high-severity findings, orange for medium, hidden when the vault has no findings. Placement moved to the header-right group, next to the settings gear. The sidebar title shortens to just "Obsilo".

## Sandbox + Tool-Execution hardening

- SandboxBridge now normalises vault paths (strips trailing slashes, maps `/` / `.` / `./` to the root) before validation, so natural agent phrasing like `ctx.vault.list('Notes/')` no longer fails with "Not a folder" and no longer drives the circuit breaker to trip.
- The circuit breaker auto-resets after a 30 s cooldown. Once-tripped sandboxes no longer stay wedged for the rest of the session.
- `read_file` retries stripped `tmp/task-*` paths against the agent-folder prefix. If the LLM forgets the `.obsidian-agent/` prefix when reading back an externalised tool result, the retry recovers transparently.
- Externalizer cleanup now retries EPERM three times with backoff before logging a warning and leaving the directory for the next plugin start to sweep. Handles the macOS iCloud transient-lock case.
- FastPath planner is tolerant of JSON wrapped in prose or markdown fences; recipes no longer drop to the normal loop because a model added a preamble.

## Community-feedback wave 4

Four fixes triggered by issue #31 from @nicholas-leonard:

- **Drag & drop from Obsidian's file explorer** attaches the file instead of opening it in a new tab. The drop handler now reads `app.dragManager.draggable` and blocks propagation so the workspace does not steal the event.
- **`vault_health_check` and `ingest_document`** are in the default tool groups. A coverage test guards against future drift.
- **Sandbox integrity hashes** for `esbuild-wasm@0.24.2` refreshed against the live jsdelivr CDN. Initial sandbox load no longer fails verification.
- **`SandboxBridge.vaultList('/')`** returns the vault root via `vault.getRoot()` instead of throwing.

## Breaking changes

None at the user level. The `/<slug>` prefix now routes strictly to skills. Workflows move to `§<slug>`, custom prompts move to `#<slug>`. The `+` button shows every registered item, so no command is gated behind knowing the right prefix.

For anyone hot-reloading Obsilo via BRAT: the BRAT-update race that blanked out the sidebar input is fixed. Settings, the mode service, and the vault-health indicator now all wait on a plugin-readiness promise before the view renders.

## Verification

- 446 / 446 unit tests pass (80+ added during the Wave-4 cycle).
- AUDIT-012 security audit: 0 Critical / 0 High. Release verdict GREEN.
- `obsidianmd/*` review-bot check: 0 findings.
- npm audit: 0 vulnerabilities across 1055 dependencies.
- Build clean.

## Install

Via BRAT:

1. BRAT -> Add Beta Plugin -> `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Manually:

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` into `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`.
2. Download and extract `obsilo-assets.tar.gz` into the same folder.

## What is next

The v2.7 cycle will pick up:

- SEC-M-1: cap HTML-comment metadata length + parseFrontmatter line count (deferred from AUDIT-012).
- SEC-L-1: regression test for the JSZip `_data.uncompressedSize` private field.
- FEATURE-2203 (skill scripts execution via sandbox) and FEATURE-2204 (coordinator sub-roles) -- the two EPIC-022 features that were kept out of v2.6 to stabilise the folder-format core first.

Deep thanks to @nicholas-leonard for the detailed bug reports that drove Wave 4.

Patch release driven by the Obsidian Community Plugin Review Bot (PR #11394, scan 5). Fixes the three Required findings and bundles a couple of WIP bug fixes for LLM providers that stringify complex tool arguments.

## Review bot fixes

- **Native dialogs replaced with Obsidian modals.** The agent-folder migration flow in Settings → Vault used `window.prompt` / `window.confirm` for the old-path input and the migration confirmation. Those have been replaced with a new `PromptModal` helper that wraps Obsidian's `Modal` + `Setting` API and returns a Promise. Cancelling resolves to `null` / `false`, Enter submits the prompt. No behaviour change beyond the dialog surface.
- **Safer provider-error stringification.** The `ExtractionQueue` permanent-error detector used a nullish fallback chain (`e?.message ?? e ?? ''`) that could stringify an object to `[object Object]` and hide the real error class. It now does an explicit `typeof` check before concatenation.

## Bundled bug fixes

- **JSON-stringified tool arguments are accepted.** Some GPT/Copilot variants serialize complex parameters as JSON strings even when the schema declares `type: "array"` or `"object"`. `inputSchemaValidator` now coerces those back in place, and `create_excalidraw` accepts both shapes for `boxes` / `arrows` so a harmless format quirk no longer hard-fails the tool call.

## What you see in the UI

Settings → Obsilo Agent → Vault → **Migrate data from old folder…**

The button still opens a two-step flow, but now through proper Obsidian modals — not native OS prompts. Behaviour is identical: type the old path, preview what's there, confirm, migrate. Originals stay in place so you can verify before deleting.

releases.description:
Community-feedback Wave 2. Defensive fixes, plugin-routing hardening, new deferred tool loading for leaner prompts.

## Highlights

### New: Deferred Tool Loading (FEATURE-1600)
24 specialised tools (office formats, diagrams, base operations, self-development helpers) are no longer in the default system prompt. The agent discovers them on demand via the new `find_tool` meta-tool — keyword query, top matches returned, schemas injected for the rest of the session. Typical reduction: 25-40% fewer prompt tokens per turn, full functionality when needed.

### Excalidraw arrows
`create_excalidraw` now supports `arrows: [{ from, to, label? }]`. Endpoint bindings stay attached when the user drags boxes in the plugin. Reference boxes by their `id` field or by zero-based array index.

### Plugin-routing hardening (BUG-018 Wave 2)
Built-in tools that are clearly superseded by an installed plugin disappear from the tool schema entirely — not just a description redirect. Current mapping: `create_excalidraw` vanishes when `obsidian-excalidraw-plugin` is enabled. `create_drawio` stays available because the drawio plugin only opens an empty editor; create_drawio delivers a finished diagram.

### Defensive provider-error handling (BUG-016)
Memory extraction and semantic-index context-prefix now pause for the session on permanent provider errors (401/402/403, "credit balance too low", "insufficient quota"). One warning, clear remediation hint, no more error-log flood on every plugin reload.

### Agent folder UI (Issue #26 follow-up)
Moved from Debug tab to Vault tab under a dedicated "Agent folder" heading. New **Pick folder...** button opens the native OS folder dialog (Finder on macOS, Explorer on Windows, Files on Linux). Folders inside the vault are saved as vault-relative paths; folders outside the vault are partially supported — the path is stored, but plugin-internal data (plugin skills, tmp results, local knowledge database) continues to live inside the vault for this release. Full cross-vault data support is planned for a later release.

## Known caveat

Changing the agent folder does not automatically move existing files or refresh the plugin's in-memory caches. Reload Obsidian after changing the path, or use the setting before you accumulate data at the old location.

- 354/354 unit tests pass (28 new for Wave 2)

releases.description:
Community-Feedback Wave 1.

## Fixes Issues #26 #28 #29 #30 + 3 Dependabot Alerts + Plugin-Routing

### Provider Robustheit
- **OpenAI-kompatible Streaming Tool-Calls** (#30) — OpenRouter gpt-oss-120b, Groq und aehnliche Backends streamen Tool-Calls mit `finish_reason="stop"`. Der neue Post-Loop-Flush fuehrt die Tool-Calls jetzt aus statt sie stillschweigend fallen zu lassen.
- **GitHub Copilot Modern Models** (#28) — gpt-5, gpt-5-codex, o3, o4-mini lehnten `max_tokens` ab. Der Copilot-Provider sendet jetzt `max_completion_tokens` fuer alle Modelle.

### Cross-Platform Korrektheit
- **Context Externalization funktioniert jetzt ueberall** (#29) — Bisher wurden grosse Tool-Results in `{vault-parent}/.obsidian-agent/tmp/` geschrieben, aber der Agent suchte sie relativ zur Vault-Wurzel, also fand er sie nie. Architektur-Fix: tmp-Files landen jetzt unter `<agent-folder>/tmp/` in der Vault. Auf Windows besonders sichtbar geworden, aber der Bug hat alle Plattformen betroffen.

### User-Kontrolle
- **Konfigurierbarer Agent-Folder** (#26) — Neues Setting unter `Settings -> Debug -> Agent folder`. Default ist `.obsidian-agent`. Plugin-Skills, Vault-DNA-Snapshot, externalisierte tmp-Results und die lokale KnowledgeDB folgen dem Setting. Kein Auto-Migration — Dateien muessen bei Pfad-Aenderung manuell umgezogen werden.

### Security (3 transitive Dependabot-Alerts)
- protobufjs ueber 7.5.5 (Critical RCE-Patch)
- hono ueber 4.12.14 (XSS-Patch)
- dompurify ueber 3.4.0 (FORBID_TAGS Bypass)
- Via npm overrides, smoke-getestet gegen Reranker, MCP-Bridge und Mermaid-Rendering.

### History-Konsistenz
- **Pairing-Fehler "tool_use ids were found without tool_result" behoben** — Anthropic und Claude-via-Copilot lehnten Konversationen ab sobald ein assistant-`tool_use` ohne matching `tool_result` in der History war. Quellen: Stream-Abbruch, Mid-Conversation-Resume, abortSignal mid-loop. Neue `sanitizeHistoryForApi` bereinigt die History vor jedem API-Call.

### Plugin-Routing
- **Excalidraw-Plugin wird erkannt und bevorzugt** — Wenn `obsidian-excalidraw-plugin` installiert ist, weist das built-in `create_excalidraw` darauf hin und leitet auf `execute_command` des Plugins um.
- **NONE-classified Plugins sind jetzt im System-Prompt sichtbar** — Plugins, die beim initialen VaultDNA-Scan keine agentifiable Commands hatten, werden unter `OTHER ENABLED PLUGINS` gelistet. Der Agent behauptet nicht mehr "dieses Plugin existiert nicht".
- **write_file-Format-Guard** — Verhindert halluziniertes Drawio/Excalidraw/Office/Canvas-Output via `write_file`. Leitet auf das richtige Built-in oder Plugin um.

### Neu: create_drawio Built-in
- **Agent-generierte Draw.io / diagrams.net Diagramme** — Neues Tool `create_drawio` erzeugt valide `.drawio` oder `.drawio.svg` Dateien mit Boxen, Rauten, Ellipsen, Pfeilen und Labels. Unterstuetzt `drawio-obsidian` und `obsidian-diagrams-net` Plugins: die `.drawio.svg` Variante rendert direkt in Obsidian und oeffnet editierbar im Plugin-Editor.

## Offene Folge-Arbeit (Wave 2)
- BUG-016: Memory-Extractor und Context-Prefix-Generator umgehen den konfigurierten Provider und rufen Anthropic direkt auf (harmlose 400-Logs fuer User ohne Anthropic-Key).
- Excalidraw-Tool-Erweiterung um echte Pfeile und mehr Shapes (aktuell: nur rectangles + text).
- Hard Tool-Filtering: built-in Tools komplett aus dem Schema entfernen, wenn ein Plugin-Aequivalent aktiv ist.

## Installation

Via BRAT:
1. BRAT installieren aus Community Plugins.
2. BRAT -> Add Beta Plugin -> `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Manuell:
1. `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/` laden.
2. `obsilo-assets.tar.gz` in denselben Ordner entpacken.

## Obsilo Agent v2.4.3

Graph intelligence, Claude remote optimization, BRAT self-provisioning.

Full release notes: https://pssah4.github.io/obsilo/releases/v2.4

### Graph intelligence

- **Confidence scoring:** Graph edges carry confidence scores (wikilinks highest, MOC medium, implicit lowest). Search results use these scores when ranking.
- **Community detection:** Louvain algorithm finds topic clusters at startup. Used by vault health checks to spot miscategorized notes.
- **God-node analysis:** Health checks flag notes with too many connections that should be split into focused hubs.
- **Knowledge freshness:** Recently modified notes get a small relevance boost in search results.
- **Batch ingest skill:** Integrate a whole folder of notes at once with entity recognition, backlinks, and frontmatter enrichment.

### BRAT self-provisioning (FIX-16/17/18/19)

BRAT installations were missing runtime assets (workers, skills, templates). All assets are now embedded in main.js and extracted on first load. CDN fallback for sql-wasm.wasm with SHA-256 integrity check.

### Claude remote improvements

- `execute_vault_op` now lists all ~33 vault operations dynamically instead of a hardcoded subset of 7
- `get_context` includes the operation list so Claude always knows what tools are available

### Vault health improvements

- Redesigned repair modal with detailed findings and selective repair
- On-demand scanning via sidebar menu
- Badge shows only repairable findings

### Security (AUDIT-010)

- Path traversal guard in AssetProvisioner
- SHA-256 integrity check for CDN WASM downloads
- Dotfile filter in build-time asset scanning

releases.description:
## Obsilo Agent v2.4.1

Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Or install manually: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` and place them in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`.

---

# Obsilo Agent v2.4.1

Introduces automated knowledge maintenance for Obsidian vaults, adds OCR support for scanned PDFs, and improves resilience for large documents and the knowledge database.

## Knowledge maintenance

Knowledge vaults grow fast but rarely stay organized. Notes accumulate without backlinks, frontmatter properties drift from the schema, categories go stale, and attachments keep their camera-roll names. Retrieval quality degrades because isolated notes are invisible to graph-based and semantic search alike.

This release adds a maintenance layer for personal knowledge management: a small number of strong hub notes (topics, concepts) that act as index pages, connected by dense backlinks to the notes they organize. When the agent ingests a new note, it searches for existing hubs first and only creates new entities when none fit. Over time this produces a navigable, well-linked knowledge graph where every note is reachable through its topic cluster and where semantic search benefits from the additional context that backlinks and frontmatter provide.

### Knowledge ingest skill

A bundled skill that guides the agent through a structured integration workflow when you say "integrate this note".

- **Entity recognition:** The agent reads the note and identifies topics, concepts, persons, projects, and organizations mentioned in the text
- **Existing-first search:** For each entity, the agent runs `semantic_search` against the vault. Existing hub notes are always preferred over creating new ones -- the explicit goal is few strong hubs, not many weak ones
- **Proposal and confirmation:** All suggestions (properties, wikilinks, MOC entries, stub notes) are presented to the user before any changes are written. Nothing happens without confirmation
- **Stub notes with content:** When a genuinely new entity is discovered, the agent creates a stub note with a short explanation, key aspects, and links back to the source -- not an empty placeholder but a starting point for further thinking
- **Source handling:** For PDFs and web clips, the agent extracts author, year, and title, proposes a filename following the configured naming convention, and populates frontmatter metadata

### Vault health check

A diagnostic and repair pipeline that runs entirely in code, without LLM calls.

- **Five check types:** orphaned edges in the knowledge graph, missing backlinks between related notes, category mismatches against the ontology, invalid wikilinks pointing to non-existent files, and general graph inconsistencies
- **One-click repair:** A modal shows all findings grouped by type. Click "Repair" and the service fixes them in batch -- backlinks are inserted, orphaned edges are pruned, categories are corrected
- **Checkpoint-backed undo:** Every repair creates a git checkpoint first. If anything goes wrong, click "Undo" to restore the exact previous state. No data loss possible
- **Status badge:** The sidebar shows a health badge with the finding count. Click it to open the repair modal

### Ontology store

A SQLite-backed store for the vault's conceptual schema.

- Stores entity clusters, relationships, and type hierarchies extracted from the knowledge graph
- Supports transitive entity association for improved linking suggestions
- Used by the health check to validate category assignments against the defined schema
- Populated automatically during semantic indexing

### Knowledge maintenance settings

Dedicated settings section for configuring knowledge maintenance features.

- **Category property name:** Which frontmatter property defines the note type (e.g. `Kategorie`, `Category`)
- **Summary property name:** Which property holds the short summary (e.g. `Zusammenfassung`, `Summary`)
- **Source naming convention:** Pattern for source file names (e.g. `Author-Year_Title`)
- **MOC properties:** Configurable properties for Maps of Content
- All settings with i18n support (German and English)

### OCR fallback for scanned PDFs

Scanned PDFs that contain only images (no extractable text) are now handled via Obsidian's text-extractor plugin.

- When `pdfjs-dist` extracts fewer than 50 characters from a PDF, the parser falls back to text-extractor's OCR cache
- The fallback reads from text-extractor's cache directory (`.obsidian/plugins/text-extractor/cache/`) -- no additional API calls
- Previously invisible scanned documents now appear in semantic search results
- Graceful degradation: if text-extractor is not installed or the file is not yet cached, the parser returns the limited pdfjs output with a hint

## Large document ingestion 

PDFs and Office documents over 50 MB can now be attached and ingested without errors. Previously, a 318-page PDF (64 MB) caused an API error because the full extracted text was embedded in the LLM context window.

- **Context truncation:** Documents larger than 80,000 characters are automatically truncated at page boundaries before being sent to the LLM. A notice tells the agent how to read remaining pages
- **Chunked reading:** `read_document` now supports `start_page` and `end_page` parameters, allowing the agent to read large documents in manageable chunks
- **Attachment support:** Both `read_document` and `ingest_document` accept an `attachment_index` parameter to read from chat attachments, not just vault files
- **Size limits:** Separate limits for images (50 MB) and documents (200 MB). `ingest_document` supports files up to 500 MB since it writes to disk, not to the context window
- **Fallback logic:** When vault file parsing fails (e.g. file too large), `ingest_document` automatically falls back to pre-parsed attachment text

### Input validation hardening

- Numeric string coercion: LLMs sometimes pass tool parameters as strings (`"0"` instead of `0`). The input validator now coerces decimal numeric strings automatically, restricted to simple notation (no scientific notation like `"1e100"`)
- Schema type enforcement: `attachment_index`, `start_page`, and `end_page` parameters use `integer` type instead of `number` to prevent float values
- XML attribute escaping: All file names and paths in attachment XML tags are now escaped to prevent injection via crafted filenames

## Database resilience 

The knowledge database (sql.js/WASM SQLite) could become corrupted by crashes during writes or iCloud sync conflicts, causing all dependent services (semantic search, graph, ontology, vault health) to fail on next startup.

- **Atomic writes:** Database saves now use a write-tmp-then-rename pattern (on native filesystem) or backup-before-write (on vault adapter), preventing half-written files from corrupting the database
- **Backup on every save:** Each successful save creates a `.bak` copy of the previous version, providing a restore point
- **Integrity check on load:** A test query runs immediately after loading the database. If the B-tree is corrupt, recovery kicks in automatically
- **Auto-recovery chain:** Corrupt primary database? Try the backup. Backup also corrupt? Create a fresh database and rebuild from vault files. No manual intervention needed
- **Stale temp cleanup:** Orphaned `.tmp` files from interrupted writes are cleaned up on startup

## Chat history action buttons

Fixed a bug where assistant messages loaded from chat history had no action buttons. Previously, only active streaming chats showed the "Insert at cursor", "Create note", "Save as Zettel", "Copy response", "Regenerate", and "Delete" buttons. Now these buttons appear on every assistant message, whether from an active chat or loaded from history.


## Other improvements

- **New chat icon:** Changed from generic plus to `message-square-plus` for better visual distinction
- **Documentation:** Added comprehensive settings reference, tools reference, troubleshooting guide, and tutorials for first conversation, getting started, and knowledge workflows

releases.description:
## Obsilo Agent **Versions 2.3.1 and 2.3.2** - released April 2026.

This release cuts token costs by up to 90% for recurring tasks, overhauls the memory and self-learning system, and fixes critical issues with the ONNX reranker and implicit connections.

## Token cost reduction

A standard task like "search my notes on Kant and write a summary" previously consumed 634,000 input tokens across 8 LLM iterations, costing roughly $2 per execution. On GitHub Copilot's 168k context limit, the agent crashed outright. After this release, the same task uses ~60,000 tokens in 2-3 iterations at ~$0.15.

### Fast path execution 

When the agent has solved the same kind of task before, it can skip the iterative ReAct loop and execute a pre-planned tool sequence instead.

- Recipe matching detects known task patterns via keyword scoring with description fallback
- A single planner LLM call produces a concrete execution plan from the matched recipe
- The plan executes deterministically: search tools in parallel (stage 1), read tools in parallel (stage 2), then 1-2 normal loop iterations for presentation
- Guardrails: hard allowlists per stage (search-only, read-only), minimum 3 successful uses before qualifying, fallback to normal loop on invalid plans
- All tool calls still pass through the governance pipeline with approval checks and logging

### KV-cache-optimized prompt structure 

The system prompt is now ordered by stability to maximize KV-cache hits across iterations.

- Stable sections (mode definition, tool descriptions, rules, security boundary) sit at positions 1-8 and are cached across all iterations within a session
- Dynamic sections (skills, memory, recipes, vault context) sit at positions 9-16
- The timestamp moved from position 1 to position 16, fixing a bug where every API call invalidated the entire cache
- A recency anchor (current task list appended as last user message) compensates for the reduced primacy of skill instructions

### Context externalization 

Large tool results no longer accumulate in the conversation history.

- Results exceeding 2,000 characters are written to temporary files with compact references in the history
- Tool-specific reference formats: search results show top 5 matches, semantic search shows top 3 with scores, read_file shows headings and a preview
- Deterministic file paths (no timestamps) preserve KV-cache compatibility
- Append-only history: externalization happens at result creation time, never retroactively
- Automatic cleanup after task completion with orphan sweep on plugin startup

## Memory and learning improvements 

### Semantic recipe promotion 

Recipe promotion switched from sequence-based to intent-based matching. The previous system compared exact tool sequences, which never worked because LLMs choose tools non-deterministically. The new system compares user messages by cosine similarity using the same embedding model as the knowledge layer.

### Budget-aware long-term memory 

The long-term memory extractor now receives the 800-character budget as a hard constraint. Each entry carries a `[YYYY-MM]` recency tag. When new information would exceed the budget, the extractor removes or condenses the least relevant entries instead of letting the file grow past the visible window.

### Session summary reliability 

Fixed a bug where session summaries were written to the database but never read back. The MemoryRetriever now queries the database directly instead of looking for legacy `.md` files that were never created.

## ONNX reranker in Electron 

The local reranker (Stage 4 of the knowledge pipeline) never worked because transformers.js detected Electron as Node.js and selected `onnxruntime-node`, whose native addon cannot load in the renderer process. Three changes fix this:

- esbuild plugin patches `IS_NODE_ENV` to `false` in the transformers.js backend selection, forcing the web/WASM branch
- WASM binary (`ort-wasm-simd-threaded.wasm`, 12 MB) is copied to the plugin directory and pre-loaded from disk to avoid CDN fetch
- RerankerService accepts `pluginAbsDir` for reliable WASM path resolution

A fail-once guard prevents repeated retries after an ONNX failure 

## Bug fixes

- **ImplicitConnections race condition :** The database could close during async computation, causing "Statement closed" errors. An `isOpen()` guard now prevents `computeAll()` from running on a closed database.
- **ChatLink YAML parse errors :** Malformed YAML frontmatter in linked notes is now logged concisely and the note is skipped instead of crashing.
- **Learned recipes force-enabled :** Recipe learning is now always active (the UI toggle was never wired up).
- **Rollup darwin-arm64:** Moved `@rollup/rollup-darwin-arm64` to optionalDependencies to fix CI builds on Linux runners.
- **Error handler lint:** Fixed `require-await` lint error in FastPath error handler callback.


---

Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Or install manually: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` and place them in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`.

releases.description:
# Obsilo Agent v2.2.8 - Release Notes

## Highlights

This release adds the **MCP Connector** for Claude Desktop and remote AI assistants, a **Unified Knowledge Layer** with SQLite-backed vector search, **local reranking**, and a comprehensive **security hardening** pass (AUDIT-006). Major internal refactoring reduces the codebase by over 7,500 lines while adding significant new capabilities.

---

## New Features

### MCP Server -- Connect Claude Desktop & Remote AI (EPIC-014)
Obsilo now acts as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, letting external AI assistants like Claude Desktop interact with your vault.

- **Local connector:** HTTP server on localhost for Claude Desktop integration via stdio proxy
- **Remote relay:** Cloudflare Workers-based relay for claude.ai, ChatGPT, and other remote clients
- **6 MCP tools:** `get_context`, `search_vault`, `read_notes`, `write_vault`, `sync_session`, `update_memory`
- **Auto session tracking:** MCP conversations appear in Obsilo's chat history automatically
- **Relay setup wizard:** One-click deployment of your personal relay worker
- **Bearer token authentication:** Auto-generated token for local server security
- **Cloudflare tunnel support:** Optional public endpoint via `cloudflared`

### Unified Knowledge Layer (EPIC-015)
The semantic search system has been rebuilt on a SQLite foundation for better performance and richer retrieval.

- **KnowledgeDB:** SQLite-backed storage (via sql.js) replacing the previous JSON file approach
- **VectorStore:** Unified vector index with cosine similarity search
- **Graph expansion:** Follow wikilinks and map-of-content properties to discover related notes (1-3 hops configurable)
- **Implicit connections:** Automatically discover semantically similar notes that have no direct link
- **Local reranking:** On-device cross-encoder model (ms-marco-MiniLM via @huggingface/transformers WASM) re-scores search results for better quality
- **Contextual retrieval:** Optional LLM-generated context enrichment for chunks (improves search quality by 49-67%)

### Storage Consolidation (FEATURE-1508)
All plugin data is now stored in a single, well-organized location.

- **Unified storage:** Global data in `~/.obsidian-agent/`, vault-specific data in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`
- **Automatic migration:** Legacy storage locations (`.obsilo-sync`, `.obsilo`, scattered JSON files) are migrated on first load
- **MemoryDB:** Episodes, recipes, and patterns migrated from individual JSON files to SQLite

---

## Security (AUDIT-006)

Comprehensive security audit with 6 high-severity findings identified and resolved:

- **MCP server authentication:** Bearer token + restricted CORS (blocks browser-based CSRF attacks) + 1 MB body size limit
- **MCP governance layer:** Path traversal prevention, IgnoreService enforcement, configDir protection, and audit logging for all MCP operations
- **Permission self-escalation blocked:** The agent can no longer silently change auto-approval settings -- an approval card is now shown
- **Backup export sanitized:** API keys and tokens are stripped before export (no more plaintext credentials in backup files)
- **Tool input schema validation:** All tool parameters are now validated against their declared schema before execution
- **SCA fix:** path-to-regexp ReDoS vulnerability resolved via npm override
- **Review-Bot compliance:** `vault.trash()` replaced with `fileManager.trashFile()` in MCP tools

---

## Refactoring & Performance

- **7,564 LOC reduction:** Major cleanup pass removing dead code, unused imports, and rendering overhead
- **UI component extraction:** `SuggestionBanner` and `OnboardingFlow` extracted from the monolithic `AgentSidebarView`
- **ModeService optimization:** Faster initialization, reduced memory footprint
- **Episodic memory defaults:** Learned recipes from episodic memory enabled by default

---

## Documentation

- Comprehensive documentation website added (VitePress)
- German language documentation for all features
- Architecture documentation updated (arc42, ADRs)

---

## Breaking Changes

- **MCP clients must authenticate:** Existing MCP integrations (Claude Desktop) will auto-reconnect via the stdio proxy worker, which reads the token from `~/.obsidian-agent/mcp-token`. Manual HTTP clients need to include `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- **Backup format:** Exported backups no longer contain API keys. Importing a v2.2.8 backup into an older version will result in empty API key fields.
- **Storage migration:** On first launch, legacy storage locations are migrated. This is automatic but one-way -- downgrading to v2.2.7 requires manual data restoration.


---

Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Or install manually: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` and place them in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo/compare/2.2.4...2.2.8

# Obsilo Agent v2.1 -- Release Notes

## Highlights

This release adds **document intelligence**, **chat-linking** for bidirectional traceability between conversations and notes, and **Office document creation** directly from the agent.

---

## New Features

### Document Parser Pipeline
Obsilo can now **read and understand** Office documents, PDFs, and data files -- both as chat attachments (drag & drop) and via the `read_document` tool.

- **Supported formats:** PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, PDF, CSV, JSON, XML
- **Drag & drop** any supported file into the chat to include its content as context
- **Semantic index** now indexes Office documents and PDFs alongside Markdown notes (when enabled)
- Pure-JS parsers (JSZip + OOXML) -- no native dependencies, works on all platforms

### Chat-Linking
Bidirectional links between your vault notes and agent conversations.

- **Manual linking:** Click the link icon on any chat in the history panel to stamp the active note's frontmatter with a deep link
- **Auto-linking on finalize:** When a conversation ends, notes that were edited during the session are automatically linked
- **Deep links:** `obsidian://obsilo-chat?id=...` opens the conversation directly in the sidebar
- **Duplicate detection:** Same conversation is never linked twice
- **Semantic titling:** Conversations get auto-generated titles via a configurable LLM model

### Office Document Creation
Three new tools let the agent create binary Office documents directly in the vault:

| Tool | Format | Library | Capabilities |
|------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `create_pptx` | PowerPoint | pptxgenjs | Slides, text, images, tables, charts, shapes, speaker notes |
| `create_docx` | Word | docx | Headings, paragraphs, tables, lists, images, page breaks |
| `create_xlsx` | Excel | ExcelJS | Sheets, cells, formulas, styles, column widths, freeze panes |

### Task Extraction
The agent can now extract actionable tasks from its own responses and create structured task notes:

- Scans for `- [ ]` checkboxes in agent responses
- Parses `@assignee` and `due: YYYY-MM-DD` annotations
- Creates notes with 10-property frontmatter (Kategorie, Status, Dringend, Wichtig, etc.)
- Smart title generation with natural phrase boundaries
- Configurable output folder

---

## Improvements

### Semantic Index Auto-Trigger
The semantic index now stays current automatically:

- **Vault events** (create, modify, delete, rename) trigger incremental re-indexing
- **Per-file debounce** (2s) prevents thrashing while typing
- **Queue-based processing** (concurrency 1) avoids API exhaustion

### Context Condensing
- Emergency condensing on 400 context overflow errors (auto-recovery)
- Improved handling of `code_execution_result` messages during condensing

### Security & Stability
- Permissive mode warning in settings when both web access and write operations are auto-approved
- Fixed `consecutiveMistakes` counter reset on mode switch (FIX-06)
- All 6 bugs from backlog resolved (FIX-01 through FIX-06)
- All security findings from AUDIT-003 addressed

### i18n
- All 6 languages (en, de, es, ja, zh-CN, hi) fully synchronized
- App: 1048 keys per language
- Homepage: 630 keys per language (Hindi was 274, now complete)

---

## Tool Count

46+ tools across 7 groups:

| Group | Count | Examples |
|-------|-------|---------|
| read | 4 | read_file, list_files, search_files, read_document |
| vault | 8 | semantic_search, query_base, create_base, update_base |
| edit | 14 | write_file, edit_file, create_pptx, create_docx, create_xlsx |
| web | 2 | web_fetch, web_search |
| agent | 12 | new_task, switch_mode, update_todo_list |
| skill | 5 | load_skill, list_skills |
| mcp | 1 | call_mcp_tool |

---

## Breaking Changes

None.

## Upgrade

Install via BRAT or replace `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` in your plugin folder.


Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → `https://github.com/pssah4/obsilo`

Or install manually: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` and place them in `.obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/`.

**Sandbox Code Execution & Self-Development**

Obsilo Agent can now execute TypeScript directly in a secure iframe sandbox. This allows the agent to independently generate files such as PPTX, XLSX, or PDF—without external tools, shell access, or manual steps.

**What's new:**

• evaluate_expression—executes TypeScript in an isolated Chromium sandbox. npm packages (pptxgenjs, xlsx, pdf-lib, d3, chart.js, and more) are automatically loaded from CDN and bundled as browser ES modules.
• Self-created skills — the agent can create its own skills as Markdown and save reusable code modules with manage_skill.
	• Dynamic Tools — using create_dynamic_tool, the agent creates new tools with TypeScript that are immediately available in the sandbox.
• Context Condensing — now enabled by default. During long sessions, the agent automatically compresses the conversation. New: 

**Security:**
Code runs in a Chromium iframe sandbox with V8 origin isolation — no access to the file system, network, or parent process. All vault operations and network access run through a controlled bridge with path validation, URL allowlist, and rate limiting.
